title: 10 best herbal capsules for eye pain
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eye pain are natural supplements that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with eye-related issues. These capsules combine various herbs to provide relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations. The best herbal capsules for eye pain include Ginkgo biloba, Euphrasia officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Achillea millefolium, Curcuma longa, Echinacea purpurea, Boswellia serrata, Cinchona officinalis, Calendula officinalis, and Centella asiatica. If left untreated, eye pain can significantly impact daily life, making it difficult to work, drive, or even perform simple tasks, ultimately affecting productivity, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with eye pain because it is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate dryness, redness, and itchiness associated with eye strain, while the antioxidant properties protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the capsules' ability to improve blood circulation may help to increase oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the eyes, further contributing to their pain-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye inflammation and oxidative stress, alleviating eye pain.

Terpenes: The terpene content of Maidenhair Tree capsules has been shown to improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help relieve eye strain, fatigue, and discomfort caused by prolonged computer use or other visual stimuli.

Ginkgo biloba extract: The extract of Ginkgo biloba leaves contains compounds that have been found to increase ocular blood flow, reduce retinal blood vessel constriction, and protect against oxidative damage in the eyes, all of which can contribute to relief from eye pain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba may help reduce eye pain by improving blood flow to the eyes and reducing inflammation in the ocular tissues.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a sesquiterpene trilactone, may help alleviate eye pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to conditions such as cataracts and glaucoma.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, may help reduce eye pain by inhibiting inflammatory pathways and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for eye health.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their flavonoid content, specifically bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation associated with eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the fall season.
Dry the leaves in a clean environment at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a grinder with 1 mm sieve size.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 1 gram of gelatin and 1 gram of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine with 500 mg capacity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with eye pain because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the body. The herb Brahmi, also known as Bacopa monnieri, has been traditionally used to improve memory and cognitive function, but it also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye strain and discomfort. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting calmness, Brahmi capsules may provide relief from eye pain caused by prolonged screen time, fatigue, or other factors.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating eye pain caused by free radical damage.

Anti-inflammatory: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eye tissues, reducing eye pain and discomfort associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or dry eyes.

Neuroprotective: Brahmi's neuroprotective properties help protect the optic nerve and promote healthy vision by reducing oxidative damage and inflammation, which can contribute to eye pain and blurred vision.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of glycosides responsible for the bioactivity of Bacopa monnieri. They help with eye pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye discomfort.
Bacopa saponins: Bacopa saponins, particularly saponins like brahmoside and bacopaside, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate eye pain caused by inflammation.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, present in Bacopa monnieri, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce eye pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bacosides, a group of compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate eye pain.
Stems: They are a rich source of bacosides, contributing to the overall efficacy of Bacopa monnieri capsules in addressing eye pain.
Roots: The roots of Bacopa monnieri are also rich in bacosides and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried bacopa monnieri plant material from a trusted supplier or grown in-house.
Grind 5-7 grams of the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground herb powder with 5-7 grams of gelatin and 10-15 ml of glycerin in a bowl.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture within 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years or label and date them properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggulu capsules helps with eye pain because they contain a powerful antioxidant-rich formula that effectively reduces inflammation and promotes healthy vision. The guggul resin extract in these capsules has been traditionally used to alleviate various eye-related issues, including conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eyes. By regulating the levels of prostaglandins and reducing oxidative stress, Guggulu capsules can help alleviate eye pain, improve visual acuity, and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Guggulu's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as guggulsterones, can reduce swelling and pain in the eyes, providing relief from conditions like conjunctivitis and uveitis.

Antioxidant properties: Guggulu's antioxidant-rich composition helps neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic eye problems like cataracts and age-related macular degeneration.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Guggulu's analgesic compounds, such as beta-elemene, have been shown to reduce pain perception, making it an effective natural remedy for acute or chronic eye pain caused by conditions like glaucoma, conjunctivitis, or dry eyes.
remedy_constituents: Guggulsterone: This type of terpene helps with eye pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to pain.
Commiphoric acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate eye pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes.
Boswellic acids: These triterpenoids have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help relieve eye pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the pain response in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules for eye pain as they contain a compound called guggulsterone, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: They are used to make capsules for eye pain as they contain a compound called guggulsterone, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for eye pain as they contain a compound called guggulsterone, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried commiphora wightii roots and stems from a reputable source.
Grind the dried roots and stems into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 250g of the powdered roots and stems with 125g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin in a bowl.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 250 empty vegetable capsules with the mixture.
Label and store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with eye pain because of their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and dryness. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting overall eye health and reducing discomfort associated with conditions like conjunctivitis, uveitis, and dry eye syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's curcuminoids, such as curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from pain.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy vision and eye function.

Pain-relieving properties: Curcumin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and discomfort in the eyes caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis, dry eye syndrome, or blepharitis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with eye pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can alleviate conditions such as conjunctivitis and uveitis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound in turmeric, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the eyes, making it beneficial for conditions like dry eye syndrome.

Ar-turmerone: Ar-turmerone, a sesquiterpene in turmeric, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye pain and reduce inflammation in conditions such as blepharitis and keratitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make capsules for eye pain as they contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation.
Stems: Stems of the plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried curcuma longa root powder from a reputable supplier.
Mix 20g of the curcuma longa root powder with 50g of a carrier substance like gelatin.
Add 10g of vitamin e oil and 2g of silica to the mixture to enhance stability.
Load the mixture into a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with 0.5g each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with eye pain because of its ability to alleviate inflammation and reduce discomfort. The herb's active compounds, such as parthenolide, have a natural anti-inflammatory effect that can soothe irritated eyes and relieve pressure. This makes it an effective remedy for conditions like conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. By reducing inflammation, feverfew capsules can help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with these conditions, promoting healthy and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's parthenolide content helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to relief from eye pain.
Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and redness in the eyes, which can contribute to relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: It helps reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and blocking the activity of certain pain receptors.
Camphor: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the eyes.
Bornyl acetate: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the eyes, as well as improve circulation and reduce pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Tanacetum parthenium are used to make capsules for eye pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Tanacetum parthenium are used to make capsules for eye pain due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Tanacetum parthenium are used to make capsules for eye pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of tanacetum parthenium flowers from a sunny location on a dry day.
Dry the flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 days.
Grind 200 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder mixture each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with eye pain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, it is absorbed into the bloodstream and can help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can be caused by conditions such as dry eye syndrome or conjunctivitis. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to have antioxidant effects, which can help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, herbal licorice capsules may provide relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or uveitis.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, which have natural analgesic and anti-edematous properties that can help numb the pain and reduce swelling in the eyes, providing relief from eye strain and irritation.

Liquiritigenin: This compound has been shown to have a direct effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce pain transmission to the brain and thus alleviating eye pain associated with conditions like dry eye or blepharitis.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps alleviate eye pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes.

Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds, including kaempferol and quercetin, contribute to eye pain relief by their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions, which help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with eye conditions.

Licoricidin: This isoflavonoid glycoside has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye pain and discomfort by modulating the immune response and protecting the eyes from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and relieve eye pain.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also a source of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm eye irritations and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier.
Clean the dried roots with a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours in a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root for 500 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with eye pain because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its mucilaginous texture forms a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, shielding them from further irritation. Additionally, marshmallow's natural soothing properties ease tension and discomfort associated with eye strain, dryness, and fatigue, providing fast and effective relief for individuals experiencing eye pain.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eye pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain, and also have an analgesic effect to relieve discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in marshmallow root capsules help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye pain and other related symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Muco-polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with eye conditions such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing pain and discomfort.
Saponins: These natural compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help combat underlying infections or irritations that may be contributing to eye pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used to make capsules for eye pain because they contain mucilages that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis are used to make capsules for eye pain because they also contain mucilages that can soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used to make capsules for eye pain because they have been traditionally used to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes and face.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of althaea officinalis root and clean it thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned root in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1/2 teaspoon of vegetable cellulose and 1/4 teaspoon of silicon dioxide in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with eye pain because they contain mucilage, a natural substance that soothes and protects the delicate tissues of the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of slippery elm help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, providing instant relief from discomfort and fatigue. Additionally, the gentle lubrication provided by the mucilage helps to nourish and calm dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, promoting healthy vision and overall eye well-being.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm contains mucilages, which are gel-like substances that can help soothe and protect the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eye pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herb contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which may help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or uveitis.

Demulcent properties: Slippery elm's demulcent properties can help create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dryness, itchiness, or foreign particles.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, including the eyes, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort associated with eye conditions.

Phenolic acids (including ellagic acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing pain and discomfort associated with eye conditions.

Tannins: These compounds have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort associated with eye conditions.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make capsules for eye pain due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for eye pain due to its potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Used to make capsules for eye pain due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh red elm bark in early spring when the sap starts flowing freely outdoors.
Dry the harvested bark in a low-temperature oven at 40c for 48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 2 parts of the powder with 1 part of gelatin and 1 part of water to form a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the herbal paste each and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with eye pain because they contain a natural ingredient that has a calming effect on the body. When taken orally, valerian root extract works to soothe inflammation and tension in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain, dryness, and irritation. This herb also promotes relaxation and reduces stress, which can exacerbate eye discomfort. By addressing the underlying causes of eye pain, herbal valerian capsules provide effective relief from chronic eye problems.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative properties can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain, by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Antioxidant activity: Valerian's antioxidant compounds can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to chronic eye pain conditions such as dry eye or macular degeneration.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic Acid: Acts as an anxiolytic and analgesic agent, reducing inflammation and pain associated with eye strain and discomfort.
Isovaleric Acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce eye irritation and alleviate pain by preventing oxidative damage to eye tissues.
Valerenol: Demonstrates sedative and muscle relaxant properties, which can help reduce eye fatigue and alleviate tension-related eye strain and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain valerenic acid, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties that help alleviate eye pain.
Leaves: They contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: They are a primary source of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce eye pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Dry the valeriana officinalis roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5 grams of the valeriana officinalis powder with 5 grams of starch in a bowl.
Fill 100-0 capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Label the capsules with the ingredients and dosage instructions clearly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes. The herbal extract contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating inflammation and related pain. Additionally, St John's Wort has antioxidant properties, which help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative damage, further reducing the risk of eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: A compound found in St. John's Wort, hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce eye inflammation and alleviate eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or episcleritis.
Hypotensive activity: St. John's Wort capsules have been shown to lower blood pressure, which may be beneficial in cases of eye pain caused by hypertension-related optic neuropathy or retinopathy.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort capsules may help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and cataracts, which can contribute to eye pain.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A terpene derivative found in Hypericum perforatum, hyperforin helps with eye pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to pain, which can alleviate eye discomfort.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound in the plant, quercetin contributes to the reduction of eye pain by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative damage and inflammation.
Hypericin: A naphthodianthron alkaloid present in the herb, hypericin can help with eye pain by its photodynamic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce eye inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating eye pain.
Flowers: They are a good source of hyperforin and hypericin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate eye pain.
Seeds: They contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help treat eye pain and other related issues.
remedy_recipe: Dry the hypericum perforatum flowers to remove moisture and preserve their potency for 24 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove lumps and large particles.
Mix 1 part of the sifted powder with 2 parts of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the powder-gelatin mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with eye pain because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in maypop have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye strain, dry eyes, and other conditions. By providing a gentle and non-invasive solution, maypop capsules can help alleviate eye pain and promote relaxation, allowing for improved vision and overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop capsules contain flavonoids and alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate eye pain by decreasing inflammation in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in maypop capsules, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain.
Pain-relieving properties: Maypop contains a chemical called alkaloid podophyllotoxin, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to relieve eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, maypop roots help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with eye pain.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, maypop barks have astringent properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: Maypop fruits contain citric acid and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve vision clarity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a low-temperature oven or dehydrator for 6 hours at 60 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part passiflora incarnata powder with three parts carrier herb like potato starch or silica gel, taking care to avoid cross-contamination.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared herbal blend, achieving a total of 500 milligrams per capsule, and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with eye pain because they contain the calming properties of lavender, which can help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles around the eyes. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds found in lavender may also help to soothe and protect the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and tension. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can promote relaxation and reduce stress, further alleviating eye strain and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lavender's anxiolytic and calming properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related eye tension and discomfort, promoting relaxation and reducing eye pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, which can be a common symptom of eye pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm eye pain.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-spasmodic effects to alleviate eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in amounts of 30g for every 100 capsules desired.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for about 10 minutes.
Mix the ground herb powder with silica gel or magnesium stearate as fillers in a ratio of 1:1 to prevent clumping.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 500mg of the herbal mixture and seal tightly for 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to one year or until opened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes. The menthol present in peppermint also has analgesic properties that numb the pain receptors, providing quick relief from eye strain and tension. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help to constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and pressure on the eyes, thereby alleviating conditions like conjunctivitis, dry eye, and headaches caused by eye fatigue.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Peppermint's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
Pain-relieving compounds: Peppermint contains compounds like menthol and methyl salicylate, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve eye pain and pressure.

Cooling and decongestant effects: The menthol in peppermint capsules can help cool and soothe the eyes, reducing redness and irritation, while also helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Leaves: Containing menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint leaves soothe itchy and irritated eyes, providing natural relief from discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source weighing approximately 250 grams.
Cleanse the harvested peppermint leaves by rinsing them with distilled water and gently patting dry with paper towels.
Dehydrate the cleaned peppermint leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours or using a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and then sift through a mesh strainer to remove any large particles weighing approximately 100 grams of powdered peppermint.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the ground peppermint powder and seal tightly for optimal storage and dosing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with eye pain because they contain a concentrated form of grape extract, rich in antioxidants and flavonoids. These powerful compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the eyes, providing natural relief from eye strain, dryness, and irritation. The capsules also work to improve circulation and oxygenation to the eyes, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration. As a result, grape capsules offer a safe and effective way to soothe and protect your eyes.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate conjunctivitis and other eye irritations that cause pain and discomfort.
Resveratrol: This polyphenol has been shown to improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate tension headaches that may radiate to the eyes and cause pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Grape roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate eye pain caused by inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the grape plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce eye irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Dry fresh vitis vinifera leaves in a cool dark place for at least 72 hours.
Grind dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 2000mg of the ground vitis vinifera powder with 500mg of organic gelatin and 100mg of glycerin.
Fill empty #00 capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately to preserve freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak capsules helps with eye pain because they contain a rich source of antioxidants, including flavonoids and tannins, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and relieving symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and blurred vision. Additionally, English oak's natural extracts help to strengthen the eyes' delicate tissues, promoting overall eye health and well-being. By supporting ocular health, these capsules can provide lasting relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: English Oak contains flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in English Oak, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and pain.

Vasodilatory effects: English Oak's flavonoids may help dilate blood vessels around the eye, improving blood flow and oxygenation to the affected area, which can aid in reducing eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English Oak contain flavonoids and terpenes, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis.
Barks: The barks of the English Oak have been traditionally used to treat eye irritations, with their astringent properties helping to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality quercus robur leaves and stems from sustainable sources weighing about 500 grams.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for five to seven days.
Grind the dried quercus robur material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered herb with three parts of potato starch and fill empty gelatin capsules weighing 500 milligrams each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper capsules helps with eye pain because they contain piperine, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed, piperine travels to the eyes and reduces inflammation, which is often a primary cause of eye pain. Additionally, piperine's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further alleviating discomfort and reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions. By targeting both inflammation and oxidative stress, herbal black pepper capsules can provide effective relief for individuals experiencing eye pain.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, the active compound found in black pepper, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce inflammation and relieve eye pain.
Antioxidant activity: Black pepper's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
Vasodilatory effects: Piperine in black pepper capsules may also help improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation and alleviating pressure-related eye pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the black pepper plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving eye pain.
Seeds: Black pepper seeds have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various eye disorders, including conjunctivitis and dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried black pepper berries piper nigrum linn and sift through a fine-mesh sieve for uniform particles.
Grind 50 grams of the sifted black pepper into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground black pepper with 500 milliliters of cold water in a glass bowl and let it infuse for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 250 milligrams of the black pepper extract and store them in an airtight container for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with eye pain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated eyes. The gel extracted from aloe vera plants has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective treatment for eye strain and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time, dry environments, or underlying conditions like conjunctivitis. By taking herbal aloe capsules, individuals can experience relief from redness, itchiness, and burning sensations in the eyes, promoting overall ocular health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or uveitis.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera contains antioxidant compounds that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue, dryness, and pain.

Wound-healing properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain gel-like substances that promote wound healing, which can be beneficial in treating eye injuries or conditions like corneal ulcers, reducing the risk of infection and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve eye strain, making them effective in alleviating eye pain.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory agents like aloin, which has been shown to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing discomfort and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from the leaves of mature plants weighing approximately 200 grams.
Crush the harvested aloe vera gel into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the crushed aloe vera gel with 100 milliliters of distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solids and extract the active compounds for 20 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the extracted liquid aloe vera solution in 500-milligram doses, using a capsule filling machine or manually pouring the mixture into gelatin caps.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried hawthorn berries crataegus monogyna and high-quality gelatin capsules.
Grind the dried hawthorn berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix one tablespoon of the ground hawthorn berry powder with enough water to form a paste.
Fill the gelatin capsules with approximately 0.5 grams of the hawthorn paste and seal them tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or freeze them for longer preservation.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye pain that are treated with herbal capsules are headaches, migraines, and facial neuralgia. Herbal supplements like feverfew, ginger, and willow bark have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the head and face. These herbal remedies can also aid in relaxing tense muscles, improving circulation, and reducing stress and tension that may contribute to these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eye pain is typically a blend of eye-friendly herbs such as Ginkgo Biloba, Bilberry, and Lutein. Ginkgo Biloba is known to improve blood circulation to the eyes, while Bilberry and Lutein help reduce inflammation and protect the retina from damage. This combination can help alleviate eye strain, dryness, and irritation, promoting overall eye health and well-being. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplement.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Italian journal of pediatrics, the fixed-dose herbal supplement significantly reduced the frequency and improved the pain intensity in patients with tension-type headache, migraine with aura, and migraine without aura. This is because the study found that the supplement reduced the frequency of headaches in patients with tension-type headache from 11.97 ± 1.92 to 5.13 ± 1.93, and improved the pain intensity from 3.06 ± 0.11 to 2.14 ± 0.19 in patients with migraine without aura. This suggests that herbal supplements, such as the one used in the study, can be effective in reducing eye pain caused by headaches.
